title: Event lines
description: This object encapsulates each security event. Each line of response body output represents one of these objects, except for the last, which is an [offset context](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/offsetcontext) object.

attackData.description: Characterizes the nature of each attack and provides details on the set of configuration rules that intercepted it. Each rule-related member encodes a conceptual array of faceted data for more than one rule. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for details.

apiId.description: For attacks on API services, this is a unique identifier under which the API is protected. It corresponds to the `apiEndPointId` value in the [API Endpoint Definition API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/api-gateway/reference/api).

apiKey.description: For attacks on API services, this is the security you specify. It corresponds to the `apiKeyName` value in the [API Endpoint Definition API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/api-gateway/reference/api).

appliedAction.description: The final action applied to the request.

clientIP.description: The IP address of the client making the request.

clientReputation.description: For Client Reputation customers, this provides data on the client IP's reputation. For example, `ID=172.19.185.64;WEBATCK=9;DOSATCK=9`. See the [Client Reputation Integration Guide](https://control.akamai.com/dl/customers/KONA/ClientReputation/ClientRep_IntegrationGuide.pdf) for details.

configId.description: Unique identifier for the security configuration that applied to this request.

policyId.description: Unique identifier for the firewall policy applied to this request. Each security configuration may contain more than one policy.

ruleActions.description: Identifies whether the request was aborted (`deny`) or allowed to pass with a warning logged (`alert`). See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

ruleData.description: User-supplied values that led each rule to trigger, typically suspect text that appears somewhere in the request, or a specified Client Reputation score. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

ruleMessages.description: The message reported by each triggered rule. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

ruleSelectors.description: Identifies the location in the request that triggered each rule, such as the name of an HTTP header. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

ruleTags.description: Represents a set of categories for the triggered rule. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

ruleVersions.description: The version of each triggered rule. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

rules.description: A series of identifiers for rules within the configuration that triggered for this request. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

slowPostAction.description: For any detected slow POST attack, this indicates the resulting action, either `W` for a warning, or `A` for abort (deny). It appears only when slow POST protection triggers.

slowPostRate.description: For any detected slow POST attack, this indicates the recorded rate of the attack in bytes per second. It appears only when slow POST protection triggers.

botData.description: An optional object that contains Bot Score data. This object is only available to Bot Manager customers on endpoints with Bot Score enabled.

botScore.description: Bot score for protected requests, expressed as a string-formatted percentage value.

responseSegment.description: Response Segment indicator: `0` - human, `1` - cautious response, `2` - strict response, `3` - aggressive response, `4` - safeguard.

clientData.description: An optional object that contains client information. This object is only available to Bot Manager customers and only on endpoints configured for bot endpoint protection.

appBundleId.description: The app bundle ID. This is present only if the telemetry type is native app SDK telemetry.

appVersion.description: The app version. This is present only if the telemetry type is native app SDK telemetry.

sdkVersion.description: Native App Traffic Protection SDK Version. This is present only if the telemetry type is native app SDK telemetry.

telemetryType.description: The telemetry type for this client request: `0` for web client standard telemetry, `1` for web client inline telemetry, or `2` for native app SDK telemetry.

custom.description: A customizable value to distinguish subsets of content. Contact Akamai Professional Services for help configuring the `custom` field. Size limit is 2KB. See [Configuration rule data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/configuration-rule) for information on decoding this value.

format.description: The format of the data representing this security event, `json` in this context.

geo.description: Encapsulates location data for the attack's source.

asn.description: The AS number or numbers that the IP belongs to.

city.description: The city to which the IP address maps.

continent.description: A two-letter code for the continent to which the IP address maps.

country.description: A two-letter ISO 3166 code for the country to which the IP address maps.

regionCode.description: A two-letter ISO 3166 code representing the state, province, or region to which the IP address maps.

httpMessage.description: Provides context on each attack's HTTP request.

bytes.description: The number of bytes served in the response, represented as a string-formatted integer.

method.description: The request's HTTP method, either `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `HEAD`, or `OPTIONS`.

path.description: The server path from the client's requested URL, excluding query strings.

port.description: The port number for the incoming request, string-formatted either as `80` or `443`.

query.description: The client request's full query string.

requestHeaders.description: The full set of URL-encoded request headers.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for each request.

responseHeaders.description: The full set of URL-encoded response headers.

start.description: A string representation of the epoch time when the edge server initiated the connection for the request.

status.description: The HTTP response status code sent to the client.

tls.description: TLS version if applicable, which corresponds to the `AK_TLS_VERSION`.

identity.description: An optional object with data about client identifiers enabled in your security configuration.

ja4.description: A JA4 TLS client fingerprint value.

tlsFingerprintV2.description: A client TLS fingerprint V2 value.

tlsFingerprintV3.description: A client TLS fingerprint V3 value.

type.description: Characterizes the source of this report data. This value is always `akamai_siem`.

userRiskData.description: An optional object that contains user risk data. This object is only available to Identity Protector customers and only on endpoints configured for identity protection.

allow.description: A value of `0` means the user wasn't on the allowlist. A value of `1` means the user was on the allowlist.

emailDomain.description: The domain name extracted from the user's email address.

general.description: Indicators, such as users per device and IPs per user, of general behavior observed for relevant attributes.

originUserId.description: The unencrypted user ID, provided by the origin.

risk.description: Indicators, such as `device`, `geo`, `asn`, `tod`, that increased the calculated risk score.

score.description: Risk score, expressed as a string-formatted percentage. A `0` means no risk, and `100` is the highest possible risk.

status.description: Status code describing any errors that occurred during risk scoring: `0` - no error, `1` - unknown error, `2` - user profile not found, `3` - user profile insufficient for accurate score, `4` - no telemetry received for Bot Manager cookie presented in this request, `5` - calculation timeout, `6` - username not found in client request, `7` - can't identify user on this non-login request.

trust.description: Indicators, such as `device`, `geo`, `asn`, `tod`, that were trusted.

username.description: The unencrypted username, provided at login by the user.

uuid.description: Unique identifier for the user whose user risk data is provided here.

version.description: The version number for this report's JSON data format.
